This isn't a standard part of CIL. TG recommends about once a day to clean the filters. We were working on creating a standard time frame and process

Everyone Just clarifying and reiterating that the two compressors being white is OK. This is NORMAL operation. The compressors are on a 10min ON 10min OFF timing and often times both compressors will be OFF at the same time. This is not an indication that there is a problem. The first identifier for problems is a FAULT or a RED compressor. Second identifier is if the pumps are white when the temperature is above 6.5. This could be a problem, usually means the system is not started. (Blue circle is compressors green is the primary temperature)

The line has stopped for maintenance; they are changing the cross conveyor!
Plain dark and milk are the exact same settings, so there is only one CL for both.
We should have separate ones since the milk program temper unit settings are different.

We do have 2 recipes, but if you observe settings are pretty much the same and we have range CLs for those, you can chose anyway, if you want separate CLs or unify the recipe on the temper unit and change it only for ZS in general.

Tempering is more consistent with dark chocolate rather than milk though. Water cooling stage is adjusted more often and ran at 12.0 or lower since we have had more issues with tempering milk. The difference between the mass set points is also a bit larger than the current allowed 1 degree deviation. I still think it would be better for clarity and consistency to have the 2 separate CL sheets. 

Alex Mendoza Brandon Lance Any thoughts?

We’re pausing CRU due to a large amount of leaking from the pipe behind C2. 

We’re looking into the situation now, and will post back.

It appears the caramel in C2 overfilled to the point it was pushing up the steam pipe. 

We’re not certain if it was forced up there by pressure, or the tank was overfilled. We’ll look further into the matter. 

For now, we’ll finish cooking C1, then scrap what’s left in C2.

I cleaned the M4 tracking roller and no more faults.

The Cavanna C6 or M6 Conveyor now has active tracking installed. It has been running for 6+ Hours without issue. The warning and fault messages are tested and working. I am not sure why this is called C6 or M6, the program, wire, and HMI have it named M13. The messages are correct when they say M13 Controls Conveyor fault.

While working on adding tracking to the control conveyor the M4 conveyor kept faulting. "output tracking warnings and faults ". It kept stopping the conveyor. I inspected and found the tracking roller is very dirty and needs added to the cleaning list or a PM.
Using paper towels to cover the pipes above the Sweco may not be the best solution, as it could create a food safety risk. Would it be possible to use caps instead? Marcos Montano Rich Brierley
